history.label: Early settlement
history.text: Archeological excavations in Tønsvika, have turned up artefacts and remains of buildings estimated to be 9,000 to 10,000 years old. Middle Ages: a fortress on the frontier The area's rich Norse and Sámi heritage.
There has also been extensive Sámi settlement on the coast south of this 'border' as well as scattered Norse settlements north of Malangen − for example, both Sámi and Norse Iron Age (0–1050 AD).

history.label: 12th century
history.text: An Icelandic source (Rimbegla) from the 12th century also describes the fjord Malangen in the south of today's Tromsø municipality as a border between Norse and Sámi coastal settlements during that part of the.
Despite only being home to around 80 people, Tromsø was issued its city charter on 20 June 1794 by King Christian VII.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: In the 19th century, Tromsø became known as the "Paris of the North".
The Diocese of Hålogaland was created in 1804, with the first bishop being Mathias Bonsak Krogh.
The city was established as a municipality 1 January 1838 (see formannskapsdistrikt law).

In 1848, the teacher training college was also moved from Trondenes Municipality (near current-day Harstad) to Tromsø, with part of its mission being to educate Sámi scholars − there was a quota ensuring that.
The teacher college was followed by the Tromsø Museum in 1872, and the Mack Brewery in 1877.
A major development was the opening of Tromsø Airport in 1964, situated on the main island, and in 1972 the University of Tromsø was opened, at the time one of four universities in Norway.
The Norwegian Polar Institute was moved to Tromsø from Oslo in 1998.
More recently, the university has expanded further through two mergers, first with University College Tromsø in 2009 and then with University College Finnmark in 2013.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: In a burial/settlement near today's airport, a sword and other combat tools have been found, as well as household tools typical for the Norwegians.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: A local teacher's college and museum were eventually incorporated into the university.
